Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Or In The Alternative To Reconsider Order Denying Petition For Order Authorizing Settlement Of Will Contest
Off Calendar Apr-26-2016 Granted Per Written Order

Petition For Order Authorizing Settlement Of Will Contest
Off Calendar Mar-09-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Phil Foster for (petitioner) Tom Mogensen.

The Court asked if Mr. Fontana was expected to appear and if Mr. Foster had further argument. Mr. Foster stated that there was motivation to settle, that they are fighting over the France property, that the decedent only owned a fraction of interest in the property, that it is out of jurisdiction, that the executor is defending the same will contest in France and that the petitioner would like to settle. The Court asked what effect would settlement have on the case in France. Mr. Foster stated that the settlement proceeds of $250,000.00 are already in California and that an order regarding the California will did not govern property in France. The Court asked what will happen if it does not approve the settlement. Mr. Foster stated that the parties would then be forced to continue litigating in France and California. The Petition for order authorizing settlement of will contest was taken under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

Petition To Revoke Probate Or, In The Alternative, For A Determination That The Decedant Did Not Intend That Her December 2011 Will Would Apply To Property She Owned In France
Off Calendar Apr-20-2015 Continued To Dec-07-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Gary Fontana (via court call) for Francois Carraud. Phil Foster for (respondent/executor) Tom Mogenson.

The Court asked if the parties had any ability to mediate. Mr. Foster stated that he client will agree to mediation, that he would like an OSC for 90 days as to why the parties will not agree, that his client has french counsel, that there is a hearing in France in June on a Motion to Dismiss which will be followed by an opportunity to appeal and that he does not want to mediate until after the June hearing. Mr. Fontana stated that he disagrees, that the petition in San Francisco is not a party to the action in France, that the hearing in June has to do with jurisdiction and the validity of the will here, that a settlement offer was made 40 days ago with no response, that they have no objection to mediation at an appropriate stage and stated that the appropriate action is to set a trial date. Mr. Fontana also stated that he does not believe that the french action will have an impact on this case but is not completely sure and that the issue in France is the validity of the french wills and property and that the action here is on the U.S. will. Mr. Foster stated that this is a mischaracterization of the french action, that portions are identical to this action, that there are the same contentions and same claims and that he understands there will be an impact. The Court suggested mediation with a completion date of September 1, 2015 and a trial date of December 7, 2015 in department 206. Discussion was held as to dates, appearances from France by telephone, interpreters and length of trial. Mr. Foster agreed to provide a good faith response to the settlement offer on or before May 15, 2015. The petition to revoke probate, etc., was referred to the department of the Presiding Judge (206) to be set for trial on December 7, 2015 , (order for the same signed and mailed to the parties).

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jun-10-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Petition for Probate or Will and for Letters Testamentary was taken under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Jones. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar May-21-2014 Continued To Jun-02-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar May-05-2014 Continued To May-21-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Phil Foster for Tom Mogensen (Petitioner). Gary Fontana for Chantal Vignes and Jacqueline Vignes (Objector).

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the issue of admitting/not admitting the 2011 Will, the effect it would have on disposition of the France property, the will contest, the Decedent's intent, etc. The Court stated that it will admit the Will to probate with a provision that there will be no disposition of the French assets until litigation is concluded. Counsel is to work on language of the provision. Mr. Foster is to prepare the order. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary was continued to May 21, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The matter will be deemed under submission on that date.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Allen. (Not Reported)
